mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 20-day forward returns following low FEDFUNDS levels are negative in more than 50% of observations, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6.0 percent signals deficient aggregate demand and weak corporate earnings growth, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in greater than 50% of months when UNRATE > 6.0, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales rise while industrial production falls, the resulting demand-supply divergence compresses corporate margins and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint interaction between RSAFS and INDPRO yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the divergence signal fails to coincide with negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, an increase in the federal funds rate reduces monetary accommodation faster than inflation declines, raising real rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 55% of observations when CPIAUCSL > 200 and FEDFUNDS rises, or Rank IC magnitude < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding capacity utilization and rising corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day S&P 500 returns following rising INDPRO occur in fewer than 55% of out-of-sample instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 850 thousand units signal weak construction activity and restrained residential investment, dragging S&P 500 returns negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in greater than 50% of months when HOUST < 850, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A high level of payroll employment combined with a low federal funds rate indicates an overheating labor market that forces rapid monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of high PAYEMS and low FEDFUNDS fails to precede negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated retail sales above 350000 million dollars reflect robust consumer spending that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when RSAFS > 350000, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
